step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the conclusion presented. One key point to add is that while mifepristone can play roles in certain gynecologic conditions unrelated to postpartum hemorrhage—such as abortion induction—it fundamentally differs mechanistically from established uterotonic agents used specifically for managing PPH. These latter drugs rely on enhancing uterine muscle function, whereas mifepristone primarily blocks hormonal signaling pathways involved in maintaining gestation rather than facilitating uterine contractions required to control bleeding. Thus, my agreement stands firm. Answer choice remains: D. Mifepristone.
